archetypes.name: The Scientist
archetypes.motivation: To prove or disprove. Knowledge is everything.

archetypes.name: The Veteran
archetypes.motivation: Seen war, survived. Hired for protection.
archetypes.vulnerability: What they've seen before doesn't prepare them for this

archetypes.name: The Journalist
archetypes.motivation: The story. Byline of a lifetime.
archetypes.skills: Asks questions others don't, records everything
archetypes.vulnerability: Will risk others for the scoop

archetypes.name: The Mechanic
archetypes.motivation: Keeps the planes flying, the radios working, the drills drilling.
archetypes.vulnerability: Rational mind that breaks when rationality fails

archetypes.name: The Patron's Agent
archetypes.motivation: Represents whoever's funding this. Has a hidden agenda.
archetypes.vulnerability: Loyalty divided between the group and the patron

archetypes.name: The Local Expert
archetypes.motivation: Whaler, sailor, guide. Knows the ice.
archetypes.vulnerability: Superstitious, or maybe just experienced enough to be afraid

archetypes.name: The Believer
archetypes.motivation: Actually read Dyer. Believes every word. Maybe obsessively.
archetypes.vulnerability: Already partially broken by what they believe is coming

npcs.name: Dr. Harold Starkweather
npcs.description: A former colleague of Dyer's who read the private account, not just the published one. Believes every word. Where Dyer concluded 'stay away,' Starkweather concluded 'go back prepared.' May be slightly unhinged by what he read.
npcs.agenda: Vindication — for Dyer, for science, for the truth. Included material in the briefing that Moore wanted cut. Has too much faith. Pushes the expedition forward when players hesitate.

npcs.name: Professor James Moore
npcs.role: Co-patron (Skeptic). Remains in the US, communicates by radio.
npcs.description: A hard-nosed empiricist brought on to keep the expedition credible. Thinks Dyer had a breakdown and that there's a rational explanation for everything. Added caveats to the briefing that Starkweather resents.
npcs.agenda: Debunking — or, if the evidence proves real, claiming the discovery for rigorous science. Keeps the expedition grounded via radio. His voice becomes increasingly distant, then silent as radio degrades.

npcs.name: The Ship Captain
npcs.description: Practical, skeptical, loyal to crew over mission.
npcs.agenda: Crew safety above all. Will resist orders that endanger the ship or its people.

npcs.name: The Radio Operator
npcs.role: The voice of the outside world
npcs.description: Maintains communication with the ship and Moore. When this voice goes silent, everything changes.
npcs.agenda: Keeping the link to civilization. Increasingly anxious as contact degrades.

npcs.name: The Dog Handler
npcs.description: The dogs are the early warning system. This person interprets their behavior — initially as weather sensitivity, later with growing unease.
npcs.agenda: Caring for the animals. The dogs know first; this person translates.

npcs.name: The Patron's Second Agent
npcs.description: There may be more than one person with a hidden agenda among the expedition members.
npcs.agenda: Secondary objectives unknown to the players. Reports to Moore or has independent instructions.

npcs.name: Echoes of the First Expedition
npcs.description: Not a living NPC. Journals, equipment, bodies, star-shaped graves. Dyer's team is present through what they left behind. Lake's private field journal with increasingly frantic entries.
npcs.agenda: The warning that went unheeded. Evidence that accumulates to destroy the 'camp disaster' story.
